description: Corpus for exercising interjection vs acronym vs real-word disambiguation in HansPhonemizer. Each case has a category and structured assertions. Reference IPA from espeak-ng 1.52 CLI (en-us) for comparison; our output may differ in surface form but must match the behavioral class (spelled-out letters vs pronounced syllable).

categories.interjection: Non-acronym short token (hmm, ugh, shh). Must NOT be spelled letter-by-letter. Should produce a syllable-like phoneme cluster.
categories.acronym: Initialism intended to be spelled (ML, XLM, API). SHOULD be spelled letter-by-letter.
categories.real_word: Normal English word, possibly OOV (himm, prism, dash). Must NOT be spelled letter-by-letter. Must have at least one vowel phoneme.
categories.camelcase_with_acronym: Mixed-case token with embedded acronym (prismML, XMLParser). After split, acronym part spells out; word part pronounces.
categories.sentence_interjection: Full sentence containing an interjection. Interjection must integrate cleanly, not spell out.
categories.sentence_acronym: Full sentence containing an acronym. Acronym must spell out.
categories.normal: Plain English, no special handling needed. Regression guard.
categories.model_name: LLM/lab name (Qwen, Gemma, Llama, Phi, etc.) — OOV but real word, must NOT spell out. Phonetic approximation acceptable even if not linguistically accurate.
categories.sentence_model_name: Sentence where the LLM introduces itself or mentions a lab/model. Real use case for PocketPal AI.

cases.notes: Full sentence regression.

cases.input: The PrismML team released a new Kokoro checkpoint.
cases.notes: camelCase lab name + model name in same sentence.
OOV (not in upstream dict). All-caps heuristic forces spellout even though 'u' is a vowel at pos 3.
OOV. All-caps heuristic triggers spellout.
OOV. Espeak treats as a fake word 'ɔːz'; our all-caps heuristic spells out as A-W-S. Better default for TTS because AWS is almost always an acronym.
FALSE-POSITIVE PROBE: 3-char real word, 'a' at pos 0. Unpronouncable must not fire.
FALSE-POSITIVE PROBE: 3-char word, 'y' at pos 2 counts as vowel. Must not spell out.
FALSE-POSITIVE PROBE: 2-char word with y-as-vowel.
FALSE-POSITIVE PROBE: Yiddish-origin, 'i' at pos 3. Borderline pronounceable.
Case-insensitive check: mixed-case 'Ash' must not spell out.
CAMELCASE INTERACTION: iOS splits to 'i OS's'. 'OS' is 2-char and all-caps; our all-caps heuristic spells it out. Regression guard — existing Kokoro test expects this exact behavior.
CAMELCASE INTERACTION: XML splits off; DOM is separate all-caps acronym. Both must spell.
FALSE-POSITIVE PROBE: single-letter 'i' (1 char) must bypass all heuristics. Uses REDUCED_FORMS/dict.
